author: barnabasbusa
content:
```

What's happening: Every buildoor reveal is rejected by lodestar's beacon API with 400: First offset must equal to fixedEnd 12 != 100 on POST /eth/v1/beacon/execution_payload_envelopes. The bid wins, the payload builds, but the envelope never gets broadcast — so every builder block finalizes without a payload. All 3 retry attempts hit the same lodestar endpoint, so nothing ever lands (buildoor's log shows Previous bid was NOT revealed — payment pending for 2 epochs every slot, and lodestar's logs show the whole network endlessly trying execution_payload_envelopes_by_root for envelopes nobody has).
```

author: nflaig
content:
a good test scenario would be
- (1) builder submit high bid to win all payloads but publishes late (eg. at second 6 into the slot)
- (2) honest proposer payload reorg forces empty slot
- (3) circuit breaker kicks in after a few slots like this and we only build local payloads

author: barnabasbusa
content: whats the circuit breaker logic ?

author: nflaig
nflaig
content: so I am scaling it by missed blocks, but generally speaking, you only count empty slots, ie. not payload revealed, and block are no longer a consideration

author: nflaig
content: also our circuit breaker is seeded at startup with some randomness, so it behaves different each time, but that we had already pre-gloas
